title: Polish the edit-tool diff render — diff stats + symbol anchor
task:
Pure renderer-side polish on the edit tool's TUI output. No tool changes, no defaults flipped, no new dependencies — uses data the tool already returns in `blast_radius`. The change is small and contained: the header sprouts a coloured stat line and the diff body gets a dim hunk-style symbol anchor.

OUTCOME — what the user sees after this lands:
- A committed edit's header reads `~ src/foo.rs  +5 -3 lines  (0.42s)` instead of `~ src/foo.rs  (0.42s)`. The `+5` is rendered in the existing green diff colour, the `-3` in the existing red diff colour. When both lines_added and lines_removed are zero (pure same-size replacement), the stat span is omitted entirely — no `+0 -0 lines` noise.
- Above the diff body (when verbosity is expanded), a dim `@@ fn target_function @@` line appears, pulling the FIRST entry from `blast_radius.containing_symbols`. It anchors the diff in the code structure at a glance, mirroring `git diff`'s hunk-header convention. When there's no containing symbol (e.g. file-level changes outside any function), the line is omitted.
- The existing `Containing symbols: fn a, fn b` line that currently appears BELOW the diff is removed. With the symbol surfaced in the hunk header, the trailing list is redundant; one anchor is plenty.
- The diff body itself is otherwise unchanged — same `+ ` / `- ` prefixes, same per-line syntect highlighting (via the existing `ContentBlock::Diff` pipeline). Just the new framing around it.

The taste-test for this render: read it at a glance, no busywork. Stats are subtle (green/red but not bright), the symbol line is dim (text only, no background, no box), and clutter is removed. Demo-friendly.

requirements.description: Add diff stats to the EditRenderer header_line for the `edit_committed` case (both the bare-success branch and the AST-override branch). Format: `~ {path}  +{lines_added} -{lines_removed} lines  ({duration})` with `+N` wrapped in `fg(GREEN, caps)` and `-N` wrapped in `fg(RED, caps)`. Numbers come from `result.blast_radius.lines_added` and `result.blast_radius.lines_removed` (already populated by the tool — see compute_blast_radius in crates/norn/src/tools/edit.rs line 461). When BOTH numbers are zero (or absent), the stats span MUST be omitted entirely — the header collapses back to `~ {path}  ({duration})`. The `edit_blocked_by_ast` and `edit_failed` headers are UNCHANGED — stats only on committed edits.
requirements.acceptance: Unit test: an edit_committed with blast_radius.lines_added=5, lines_removed=3 produces a header containing `+5` (with the green SGR) and `-3` (with the red SGR) and the literal ` lines `. A separate test: an edit_committed with both lines_added=0 and lines_removed=0 produces a header that does NOT contain `+0`, `-0`, or the word `lines`. The blocked and failed branches' tests pass unchanged. cargo test -p norn-tui passes; cargo clippy -p norn-tui --all-targets -- -D warnings clean.

requirements.description: Add a dim symbol-anchor hunk header above the diff body in EditRenderer::body_blocks. The new ContentBlock returned for an `edit_committed` becomes a TWO-block sequence: first a ContentBlock::Plain carrying `\x1b[2m@@ {symbol} @@\x1b[22m\n` (dim SGR, no colour), then the existing ContentBlock::Diff. `{symbol}` is `result.blast_radius.containing_symbols[0].as_str()` — the FIRST entry only. When containing_symbols is missing, empty, or its first element is not a string, the Plain hunk-header block is OMITTED and body_blocks returns just the Diff block as today. The `edit_committed` with `has_overrides(result) == true` branch must also emit the hunk header (mirror the same containing_symbols lookup); the existing diagnostics-tail behaviour from body() is unchanged because body_blocks already short-circuits the body() path when it returns Some.
requirements.acceptance: Unit test: an edit_committed with containing_symbols=["fn target_function"] produces a body that, when piped through render_blocks, contains `@@ fn target_function @@` wrapped in dim SGR markers (`\x1b[2m` ... `\x1b[22m`) and appears BEFORE the first `+` or `-` diff line. A second test: an edit_committed with no containing_symbols field, or with containing_symbols=[], produces a body that does NOT contain the `@@` marker. The AST-override branch test (edit_override_header_and_body) also surfaces the hunk header when containing_symbols is present.

requirements.description: Remove the old `Containing symbols: fn a, fn b` line from EditRenderer::body. The function `containing_symbols_line` and its single call site (inside the `edit_committed` arm of body() at edit.rs around line 107) are removed. With R2's hunk anchor surfacing the first symbol, the trailing redundant list is gone. body() for `edit_committed` returns just the colourised diff (and, on the override branch, the diff + diagnostics). NOTE that body() is the fallback path consumed when body_blocks returns None — under R2 it does NOT return None for edit_committed, so body() is only reached on the failed/blocked/override branches today. The override branch still emits diagnostics; just no symbols list.
requirements.acceptance: Unit test: the existing `edit_success_body_has_diff_and_symbols` test must be UPDATED (rename `_and_symbols` → just `_diff`) to assert the diff lines but NOT the `Containing symbols:` string. Grep for `containing_symbols_line` returns zero hits in production code. cargo test -p norn-tui passes.

requirements.description: All existing edit-renderer tests must still pass after the changes in R1+R2+R3 (with the one rename noted in R3). Verification gates: cargo check -p norn-tui; cargo clippy -p norn-tui --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test -p norn-tui; grep TODO/FIXME/HACK in changed files returns zero hits.
requirements.acceptance: All four cargo commands succeed clean. The renderer test module in crates/norn-tui/src/tools/rich/edit.rs has at least: edit_committed_header_with_stats, edit_committed_header_without_stats_when_zero, edit_committed_body_has_hunk_header_when_symbol_present, edit_committed_body_no_hunk_header_when_symbols_absent, edit_blocked_header_and_body (unchanged), edit_override_header_and_body (updated to assert hunk header), edit_failed_header_no_body (unchanged).

context:
FILES:
- crates/norn-tui/src/tools/rich/edit.rs — primary file. EditRenderer::header_line is around line 55. body_blocks is around line 129. body() is around line 94. containing_symbols_line is around line 41 — this is the function to remove in R3. The tests module starts around line 164.
- crates/norn-tui/src/render/content.rs — ContentBlock enum + render_blocks. ContentBlock::Plain and ContentBlock::Diff are both already defined. The render_blocks loop iterates blocks in order — Plain blocks pass through verbatim, Diff blocks call render_diff(). Returning a [Plain, Diff] vec naturally renders Plain first.
- crates/norn-tui/src/tools/helpers.rs — provides fg(GREEN, caps), fg(RED, caps), fg_reset(), dim(), reset(). GREEN = RgbColor::new(80, 180, 80). RED = RgbColor::new(200, 80, 80). Both already used elsewhere in the renderer. dim() = `\x1b[2m`, reset() / fg_reset() the matching `\x1b[22m` or full SGR reset.
- crates/norn/src/tools/edit.rs — the source-of-truth for the result JSON shape. compute_blast_radius (line 461) constructs the blast_radius object: lines_added, lines_removed, lines_modified, containing_symbols are all already there. R1 + R2 just read these fields defensively.

WHAT'S ALREADY CORRECT — DO NOT REIMPLEMENT:
- The edit tool's blast_radius computation — lines_added/removed/modified and containing_symbols are populated correctly by the tool.
- The ContentBlock::Diff renderer (render_diff in content.rs) — per-side syntect highlighting, red/green +/- prefixes. Keep using it as-is.
- The header layout for edit_blocked_by_ast and edit_failed cases. Untouched.
- The streaming_header. Untouched.
- AST override warning glyph (`⚠`) and amber treatment in the override branch — preserve, just add the stats inside that header too.
- The diagnostics rendering in body() for the override and blocked branches.

DESIGN TASTE:
- Stats colour MUST use the existing GREEN / RED constants from helpers.rs — no new colours, no new RGB values. Match the diff body's own +/- colours so the eye reads them as the same idea.
- Hunk header MUST be dim only — no background, no boxes, no extra glyphs. The text `@@ fn name @@` in dim is the entire visual; that's how git diff does it and it reads beautifully in any terminal.
- The blast_radius stat reads `+5 -3 lines` (with a single trailing word `lines`), NOT `+5 lines -3 lines` and NOT `+5 / -3`. Mirror the standard git-diff stat-line convention.
- When stats are zero, ELIDE the entire stats span including the leading double-space. The header is `~ src/foo.rs  ({duration})` — same as today. No `+0 -0` ghosts.

DIFFY / OTHER DEPS:
- The renderer already uses diffy (via colourise_unified_diff for ApplyPatch — separate path) and syntect (via render_blocks). NO new deps are needed for this task. Do not add a new crate.

STANDARDS — per /Users/tom/Developer/ablative/yggdrasil/CLAUDE.md: no unwrap/expect/panic/todo in production code (defensive field access with and_then/unwrap_or already used throughout edit.rs — keep that style); pedantic clippy clean; no TODO/FIXME/HACK; thiserror in library code (not relevant here, no error types added); files under 500 LOC (edit.rs is well under).

VERIFICATION: cargo check -p norn-tui; cargo clippy -p norn-tui --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test -p norn-tui; grep -nE 'TODO|FIXME|HACK|unimplemented!' crates/norn-tui/src/tools/rich/edit.rs returns no hits.
